---
title: "Outputs"
author: "Simon E. Lobos"
date: "June, 20267"
output: html_document
---

```{r include = TRUE, message=FALSE, eval = FALSE} 
#Code adapted from: van Rensburg et al. (2018). https://doi.org/10.1101/427872
#                   Luqman et al. (2023). https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-023-36631-9
#                   Gallegos et al. (2023). https://doi.org/10.1111/mec.16848

setwd("~/Desktop/Comparative-Landscape-Genomics/4_Outputs") 

library(gradientForest)
library(dplyr)
library(calibrate)
library(ggplot2)
library(raster)
library(sf)
library(reshape2)
library(tmap)
library(basicPlotteR)
library(tmaptools)
library(vegan)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(colorspace)
library(shadowtext)
library(forcats)
library(terra)
library(tidyterra)  
library(tidyterra)
library(ggrepel)
```

Outcomes of gradient forest models (Figure 2A)
```{r }
#Create a relative importance heatmap for each environmental predictor variable for each species.

#Load gradientForest results
load("~/Desktop/Comparative-Landscape-Genomics/3_GEA/3_3_gradientForest/3_3_1_BM/BM_GF_BP_LF.RData") #B. marginata
load("~/Desktop/Comparative-Landscape-Genomics/3_GEA/3_3_gradientForest/3_3_1_CR/CR_GF_BP_LF.RData") #C. rhomboidea  
load("~/Desktop/Comparative-Landscape-Genomics/3_GEA/3_3_gradientForest/3_3_1_EI/EI_GF_BP_LF.RData") #E. impressa
load("~/Desktop/Comparative-Landscape-Genomics/3_GEA/3_3_gradientForest/3_3_1_EO/EO_GF_BP_LF.RData") #E. obliqua
load("~/Desktop/Comparative-Landscape-Genomics/3_GEA/3_3_gradientForest/3_3_1_GA/GA_GF_BP_LF.RData") #G. aquifolium
load("~/Desktop/Comparative-Landscape-Genomics/3_GEA/3_3_gradientForest/3_3_1_XA/XA_GF_BP_LF.RData") #X. australis

#Retrieve R2 importance values of the evn. predictors 
R.sq.alldatasets <- (rowMeans(ban.BP.lfmm.GF$imp.rsq, na.rm=T)) 
R.sq.alldatasets <- as.data.frame(R.sq.alldatasets)
R.sq.alldatasets
colnames(R.sq.alldatasets) <- "BM"

R.sq.CR <- (rowMeans(cal.BP.lfmm.GF$imp.rsq, na.rm=T))
R.sq.CR <- as.data.frame(R.sq.CR)
R.sq.CR
R.sq.alldatasets$CR <- R.sq.CR$R.sq.CR

R.sq.EI <- (rowMeans(epa.BP.lfmm.GF$imp.rsq, na.rm=T))
R.sq.EI <- as.data.frame(R.sq.EI)
R.sq.alldatasets$EI <- R.sq.EI$R.sq.EI

R.sq.EO <- (rowMeans(euc.BP.lfmm.GF$imp.rsq, na.rm=T))
R.sq.EO <- as.data.frame(R.sq.EO)
R.sq.alldatasets$EO <- R.sq.EO$R.sq.EO

R.sq.GA <- (rowMeans(gre.BP.lfmm.GF$imp.rsq, na.rm=T))
R.sq.GA <- as.data.frame(R.sq.GA)
R.sq.alldatasets$GA <- R.sq.GA$R.sq.GA

R.sq.XA <- (rowMeans(xan.BP.lfmm.GF$imp.rsq, na.rm=T))
R.sq.XA <- as.data.frame(R.sq.XA)
R.sq.alldatasets$XA <- R.sq.XA$R.sq.XA

R.sq.alldatasets <- as.matrix(R.sq.alldatasets) %>% round(digits = 3) #turn into matrix for heatmap

#Change column names 
heatmap.col.names <- c("BA","CA","EP","EU","GR","XA")

colnames(R.sq.alldatasets) <- heatmap.col.names

R.sq.all.melt <- melt(R.sq.alldatasets)  #melt for ggplot heatmap
R.sq.all.melt <- R.sq.all.melt[order(R.sq.all.melt$Var1),] #order by predictor variable
write.csv(R.sq.all.melt, file="Heatmap.R.sq.all.melt.spp.csv")

#Set colour pallete
pal7 <- colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(9,"Blues"))(100)

#Plot
pal7 <- colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(9,"Blues"))(100)

ggplot(R.sq.all.melt, aes(x=Var2, y=fct_inorder(Var1), fill=value)) + 
  coord_equal() +
  ylab('Environmental variables') + 
  xlab('Species') +
  geom_tile(colour="grey30", size=0.005) + 
  #geom_text(aes(label=value), size=6) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colours = pal7) +
  theme_test() +
  theme(legend.position="bottom", legend.box = "horizontal",
        legend.title = element_text(size = 18),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.text.x = element_text(colour="black", size=18, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 0.5),
        axis.text.y = element_text(colour="black", size=18, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 0.5),
        axis.title.y = element_text(colour="black", size=18, face="plain"),
        axis.title.x = element_text(colour="black",size=18,face="plain")) +
  guides(fill = guide_colorbar(title = expression(paste(italic("R"^"2")," weighted importance")),
                               label.position = "bottom",
                               title.position = "left", title.vjust = 0.8,
                               frame.colour = "black",
                               barwidth = 10,
                               barheight = 1.5,
                               ticks.colour = "grey30")) 

ggsave("Heatmap.GF.R2.spp_Rvalues.pdf", width = 6.5, height = 6.5, dpi = 300)
```

Outcomes of gradient forest models (Figure 2B)
```{r }
#load GF cumulative importance dfs of each species 
ban.cand.preds <- load("~/Desktop/Comparative-Landscape-Genomics/3_GEA/3_3_gradientForest/3_3_1_BM/bm.cand.preds.df.RData") 
cal.cand.preds <- load("~/Desktop/Comparative-Landscape-Genomics/3_GEA/3_3_gradientForest/3_3_1_CR/cr.cand.preds.df.RData") 
epa.cand.preds <- load("~/Desktop/Comparative-Landscape-Genomics/3_GEA/3_3_gradientForest/3_3_1_EI/ei.cand.preds.df.RData") 
euc.cand.preds <- load("~/Desktop/Comparative-Landscape-Genomics/3_GEA/3_3_gradientForest/3_3_1_EO/eo.cand.preds.df.RData") 
gre.cand.preds <- load("~/Desktop/Comparative-Landscape-Genomics/3_GEA/3_3_gradientForest/3_3_1_GA/ga.cand.preds.df.RData") 
xan.cand.preds <- load("~/Desktop/Comparative-Landscape-Genomics/3_GEA/3_3_gradientForest/3_3_1_XA/xa.cand.preds.df.RData") 

all.cand.preds <- rbind(bm.cand.preds.df, cr.cand.preds.df, ei.cand.preds.df, 
                        eo.cand.preds.df, ga.cand.preds.df, xa.cand.preds.df) %>%
  mutate(x = as.numeric(x))

y.max <- max(all.cand.preds$y)

coluors <- c("#CC79A7","#43BA8F","#7DCCFF",
             "#F09163","#97CE2F","#B7B7B7")

all.cand.preds$pred <- factor(all.cand.preds$pred, levels = c("TWI", "AVP", "pHc","BIO05"))

colnames <- c(TWI = "TWI",
              AVP = "SAP (mg/kg)",
              pHc = "SpH",
              BIO05 = "TMAX (°C)")

all.cand.preds %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_step(aes(x=x,y=y,color=snps, linetype = snps), size = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = coluors) +
  facet_wrap(~ pred, scales = "free_x", labeller = as_labeller(colnames)) + 
  scale_linetype_manual(name = "snps", 
                        values = c(bm.cand = "solid", cr.cand = "solid", 
                                   ei.cand = "solid", eo.cand = "solid", 
                                   ga.cand = "solid", xa.cand = "solid")) +
  ylab("Cumulative Importance") +
  xlab("Predictor value") +
  ylim(c(0,y.max))+
  theme_bw()+
  theme(#legend.position = "none", 
    strip.text = element_text(size = 15),
    axis.text.x = element_text(colour="black",angle = 0, 
                               vjust = 0.5, hjust = 0.5, size=13),
    axis.text.y = element_text(colour="black",size=13,face="plain"),
    axis.title.x = element_text(colour="black",size=18,face="plain"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(colour="black",size=18,face="plain"),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank())   

save.image(file="GF_CI_spp.RData")

ggsave("GF_CI_spp.pdf", width = 8, height = 7, dpi = 300)
```

Mapping putative adaptive variation across geographic space (Figure 3)
```{r }
#Load env. raster stack
load("~/Desktop/Comparative-Landscape-Genomics/Environmental_data/Env.stack.2.RData")

Env.stack.2.grid <- data.frame(TWI = values(Env.stack.2[["TWI"]]), 
                               AVP = values(Env.stack.2[["AVP"]]), 
                               pHc = values(Env.stack.2[["pHc"]]), 
                               BIO05 = values(Env.stack.2[["BIO05"]]))
```

Project gradient forest model in space
```{r Banksia marginata}
#Transform gradient forest predictions
ban.vars <- names(importance(ban.BP.lfmm.GF))
BM.grid.pre <- predict(ban.BP.lfmm.GF, Env.stack.2.grid[complete.cases(Env.stack.2.grid), ban.vars])

#PCA
BM.PCA <- prcomp(na.omit(BM.grid.pre), center=TRUE, scale.=FALSE)
save(BM.PCA, file="BM.PCA.RData")
BM.pcs.full.gf <- BM.PCA 
summary(BM.PCA)

#Assign PC
BM.a1 <- BM.PCA$x[, 1]
BM.a2 <- BM.PCA$x[, 2]
BM.a3 <- BM.PCA$x[, 3]

#Define RGB colour palette (your choice)
BM.r <- BM.a1 + BM.a2
BM.g <- -BM.a2
BM.b <- BM.a3 + BM.a2 - BM.a1
#Scale colors
BM.r <- (BM.r - min(BM.r))/(max(BM.r) - min(BM.r)) * 255
BM.g <- (BM.g - min(BM.g))/(max(BM.g) - min(BM.g)) * 255
BM.b <- (BM.b - min(BM.b))/(max(BM.b) - min(BM.b)) * 255

BM.nvs <- dim(BM.PCA$rotation)[1]
BM.vec <- ban.vars
BM.lv <- length(BM.vec)
BM.vind <- rownames(BM.PCA$rotation) %in% BM.vec
scal <- 20
BM.xrng <- range(BM.PCA.vif$x[, 1], BM.PCA.vif$rotation[, 1]/scal) * 1.1
BM.yrng <- range(BM.PCA.vif$x[, 2], BM.PCA.vif$rotation[, 2]/scal) * 1.1
BM.trns_site <- predict(ban.BP.lfmm.GF.vif)
BM.PCA.sites <- predict(BM.PCA.vif, BM.trns_site[, ban.vars])
jit <- 0.001

write.csv(BM.PCA.sites, file="BM.PCA.sites.csv")
BM.PCA.sites_names <- read.csv("BM.PCA.sites.csv") # This dataset is the same as "BM.PCA.sites" but includes an additional column with updated population names. The population names were simplified by replacing long labels with numerical identifiers.

tiff("BM_Biplot_biological_space_GF_BP_LF_2.tiff", width=1500, height=1300, res=300)
par(mar = c(2, 2, 0.2, 0.5), mgp = c(0, 0, 0)) 
plot((BM.PCA$x[, 1:2]), xlim = BM.xrng, ylim = BM.yrng, xlab = "PC1 (65%)", ylab = "PC2 (19%)", 
     pch = ".", cex = 4, col = rgb(BM.r, BM.g, BM.b, max = 255), 
     asp = 1, cex.lab = 1.1, cex.axis = 1, frame.plot = FALSE, axes=FALSE, col.lab="white")
#rect(par("usr")[1], par("usr")[3],par("usr")[2], par("usr")[4])
#par(new = TRUE)
#abline(h = 0, v = 0, col = "grey70", lwd = 1)
#axis(1, pos = 0, col = "grey70", labels = F, tck = 0) 
#axis(2, pos = 0, col = "grey70", labels = F, tck = 0)
points(BM.PCA.sites_names[, 3:4],  pch = c(25,
                                           24,
                                           22,
                                           24,
                                           24,
                                           24,
                                           21,
                                           24,
                                           24,
                                           23,
                                           22,
                                           23,
                                           22,
                                           23,
                                           23,
                                           9,
                                           9,
                                           23,
                                           21,
                                           21,
                                           21,
                                           22,
                                           25,
                                           23,
                                           24,
                                           24,
                                           25,
                                           25,
                                           22,
                                           22), lwd = 0.7, cex = 1, 
       bg = NA, 
       col = "black")
text(BM.PCA$rotation[BM.vec, 1]/scal + jit * sign(BM.PCA$rotation[BM.vec, 1]), 
     BM.PCA$rotation[BM.vec, 2]/scal + jit * sign(BM.PCA$rotation[BM.vec, 2]), 
     labels = NULL,  cex = 0.5, pos = 2)
arrows(rep(0, BM.lv), rep(0, BM.lv), 
       BM.PCA$rotation[BM.vec, 1]/scal, 
       BM.PCA$rotation[BM.vec, 2]/scal, 
       length = 0.1, lwd = 3.5, angle = 20, 
       col = "black")
arrows(rep(0, BM.lv), rep(0, BM.lv), 
       BM.PCA$rotation[BM.vec, 1]/scal, 
       BM.PCA$rotation[BM.vec, 2]/scal, 
       length = 0.1, lwd = 2, angle = 20, 
       col = c("#B7B7B7","#CC79A7","#B78560","#43BA8F"))
#labels = BM.vec,  cex = 0.5, pos = 2)
#addTextLabels(BM.PCA.sites_names[, 3], 
#BM.PCA.sites_names[, 4], 
#BM.PCA.sites_names$Pop ,
#col.background=rgb(0,0,0,0.75),
#cex.label=0.7, col.label="white")
dev.off()

#Map predicted genetic turnover in geographic space
Env.stack.2.grid$x <- 1:nrow(Env.stack.2.grid)
Env.stack.2.grid$nas <- complete.cases(Env.stack.2.grid)
BM.pcs <- as.data.frame(BM.PCA$x)
BM.pcs$x <- Env.stack.2.grid$x[Env.stack.2.grid$nas]
BM.pcs <- merge(BM.pcs, Env.stack.2.grid, by = "x", all = TRUE)
BM.pcs$r <- BM.pcs$PC1 + BM.pcs$PC2
BM.pcs$g <- -BM.pcs$PC2
BM.pcs$b <- BM.pcs$PC3 + BM.pcs$PC2 - BM.pcs$PC1
BM.pcs$r <- (BM.pcs$r - min(BM.pcs$r, na.rm = TRUE))/(max(BM.pcs$r, na.rm = TRUE) - min(BM.pcs$r, na.rm = TRUE)) * 255
BM.pcs$g <- (BM.pcs$g - min(BM.pcs$g, na.rm = TRUE))/(max(BM.pcs$g, na.rm = TRUE) - min(BM.pcs$g, na.rm = TRUE)) * 255
BM.pcs$b <- (BM.pcs$b - min(BM.pcs$b, na.rm = TRUE))/(max(BM.pcs$b, na.rm = TRUE) - min(BM.pcs$b, na.rm = TRUE)) * 255
BM.raster.r <- BM.raster.g <- BM.raster.b <- Env.stack.2[[1]]
values(BM.raster.r) <- BM.pcs$r
values(BM.raster.g) <- BM.pcs$g
values(BM.raster.b) <- BM.pcs$b
BM.rgbmap <- stack(BM.raster.r, BM.raster.g, BM.raster.b)

#Load coordinates
BM.coords <- read.csv("~/Desktop/Comparative-Landscape-Genomics/Environmental_data/BM_coords_Ch2.csv")

##Plot using plotRGB function
png("BM_Mapping_geographic_space_GF_BP_LF.png", width=800, height=1550, res=200)
plotRGB(BM.rgbmap)
points(BM.coords$lon, BM.coords$lat,pch = 21, lwd = 1.5, cex = 1, bg = "white")
dev.off()

##Plot using ggplot2 function

#Load sampling locations shapefile 
BM.coords.shp=st_read("~/Desktop/Comparative-Landscape-Genomics/Environmental_data/BM_coords_Ch2.shp")

#Load shapefile of Gariwerd boundary
gnp <- st_read("~/Desktop/Comparative-Landscape-Genomics/Environmental_data/Grampians_Wh_2.shp")

#Convert the Pop column into a factor
BM.coords.shp$Pop <- factor(
  BM.coords.shp$Pop,
  levels = unique(BM.coords.shp$Pop))

#Convert BM.rgbmap into a terra SpatRaster object
BM.rgbmap.spat <- rast(BM.rgbmap)

#Plot
ggplot() +
  geom_spatraster_rgb(data = BM.rgbmap.spat) +
  geom_sf(data = gnp, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.5) +  
  geom_point(data = BM.coords.shp, aes(x = lon, y = lat, shape = Pop),
             size = 10, colour = "black", fill = "white",stroke = 1.5) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(25,
                                24,
                                22,
                                24,
                                24,
                                24,
                                21,
                                24,
                                24,
                                23,
                                22,
                                23,
                                22,
                                23,
                                23,
                                9,
                                9,
                                23,
                                21,
                                21,
                                21,
                                22,
                                25,
                                23,
                                24,
                                24,
                                25,
                                25,
                                22,
                                22)) +  
  geom_label_repel(data = BM.coords.shp, aes(x = lon, y = lat, label = Pop),
                   color = "black", 
                   fontface = "bold",
                   segment.color = "grey30",
                   segment.size = 1,
                   min.segment.length = 0.1,
                   na.rm = TRUE,
                   show.legend = TRUE,
                   size = 10,
                   box.padding = 1,        
                   point.padding = 0.5,     
                   force = 2,
                   max.overlaps = Inf) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) 

ggsave(file = "BM_GF_map.pdf", width = 12, height = 12, dpi = 300)
```

```{r Callitris rhomboidea}
##load GF object (cal.BP.lfmm.GF) 
 
load("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/2_Chapter/2_3_DataAnalysis/gradientForest_Combined/BP_LF/CR/CR_GF_BP_LF.RData") 

#PCA of the transformed grid for plotting in RGB palette}

#The multi-dimensional biological space can most effectively be represented by taking the principle components of the transformed grid and presenting the first two dimensions in a biplot.

# To allow straightforward Procrustes superpositioning and generation of ensemble model, I only calculate the first 8 PCs here. This is perfectly fine because I anyway only visualise the first 3 PCs. 
#Reference: https://github.com/hirzi/RhEA/blob/main/Gradient%20forest%20and%20glacial%20genomic%20offset/GradientForest_GlacialGenomicOffset.R#L361

# Transform variables by predicting with GF model
cal.vars <- names(importance(cal.BP.lfmm.GF))
CR.grid.pre <- predict(cal.BP.lfmm.GF, Env.stack.2.grid[complete.cases(Env.stack.2.grid), cal.vars]) # need to get rid of NA rows or an error pops up

# CR.PCA of the transformed grid for plotting in RGB palette
CR.PCA <- prcomp(na.omit(CR.grid.pre), center=TRUE, scale.=FALSE)
save(CR.PCA, file="CR.PCA.RData")
CR.pcs.full.gf <- CR.PCA # Output for ensemble model generation
#Standard deviations (1, .., p=4):
#  [1] 0.013668758 0.008437451 0.008094986 0.004026576

#Rotation (n x k) = (4 x 4):
#             PC1        PC2       PC3         PC4
#TWI    0.5956679 -0.7598499 0.2595078  0.02153203
#AVP   -0.5844038 -0.1890759 0.7758388  0.14421067
#BIO05  0.4078885  0.4342836 0.2726631  0.75543333
#pHc    0.3705116  0.4452822 0.5063445 -0.63879586

summary(CR.PCA)
#Importance of components:
#                           PC1      PC2      PC3      PC4
#Standard deviation     0.01367 0.008437 0.008095 0.004027
#Proportion of Variance 0.54989 0.209530 0.192860 0.047720
#Cumulative Proportion  0.54989 0.759420 0.952280 1.000000

CR.a1 <- CR.PCA$x[, 1]
CR.a2 <- CR.PCA$x[, 2]
CR.a3 <- CR.PCA$x[, 3]
CR.r <- CR.a1 + CR.a2
CR.g <- -CR.a2
CR.b <- CR.a3 + CR.a2 - CR.a1
# Scale colors
CR.r <- (CR.r - min(CR.r))/(max(CR.r) - min(CR.r)) * 255
CR.g <- (CR.g - min(CR.g))/(max(CR.g) - min(CR.g)) * 255
CR.b <- (CR.b - min(CR.b))/(max(CR.b) - min(CR.b)) * 255

# Plot predicted genetic turnover in genetic space (CR.PCA biplot)
CR.nvs <- dim(CR.PCA$rotation)[1]
CR.vec <- cal.vars
CR.lv <- length(CR.vec)
CR.vind <- rownames(CR.PCA$rotation) %in% CR.vec
scal <- 20
CR.xrng <- range(CR.PCA$x[, 1], CR.PCA$rotation[, 1]/scal) * 1.1
CR.yrng <- range(CR.PCA$x[, 2], CR.PCA$rotation[, 2]/scal) * 1.1
CR.trns_site <- predict(cal.BP.lfmm.GF)
CR.PCA.sites <- predict(CR.PCA, CR.trns_site[, cal.vars])
jit <- 0.001

#write.csv(CR.PCA.sites, file="CR.PCA.sites.csv")
CR.PCA.sites_names <- read.csv("CR.PCA.sites.csv") 

#tiff("CR_Biplot_biological_space_GF_BP_LF_2_labels.tiff", width=1500, height=1300, res=300)
#tiff("CR_Biplot_biological_space_GF_BP_LF_2_vec.tiff", width=1500, height=1300, res=300)
tiff("CR_Biplot_biological_space_GF_BP_LF_2.tiff", width=1500, height=1300, res=300)
par(mar = c(2, 2, 0.2, 0.5), mgp = c(0, 0, 0)) 
plot((CR.PCA$x[, 1:2]), xlim = CR.xrng, ylim = CR.yrng, xlab = "PC1 (55%)", ylab = "PC2 (21%)", 
     pch = ".", cex = 4, col = rgb(CR.r, CR.g, CR.b, max = 255), 
     asp = 1, cex.lab = 1.1, cex.axis = 1, frame.plot = FALSE, axes=FALSE, col.lab="white")
#rect(par("usr")[1], par("usr")[3],par("usr")[2], par("usr")[4])
#par(new = TRUE)
#plot((CR.PCA$x[, 1:2]), xlim = CR.xrng, ylim = CR.yrng, xlab = "PC1 (55%)", ylab = "PC2 (21%)", 
#     pch = ".", cex = 4, col = rgb(CR.r, CR.g, CR.b, max = 255), 
#     asp = 1, cex.lab = 2, cex.axis = 1)
#abline(h = 0, v = 0, col = "grey70", lwd = 1)
#axis(1, pos = 0, col = "grey70", labels = F, tck = 0) 
#axis(2, pos = 0, col = "grey70", labels = F, tck = 0)
points(CR.PCA.sites_names[, 3:4],  pch = c(24,
                                           22,
                                           24,
                                           24,
                                           24,
                                           21,
                                           24,
                                           24,
                                           23,
                                           22,
                                           23,
                                           22,
                                           23,
                                           23,
                                           9,
                                           9,
                                           23,
                                           21,
                                           21,
                                           21,
                                           22,
                                           25,
                                           23,
                                           24,
                                           24,
                                           25,
                                           25,
                                           22), lwd = 0.7, cex = 1, 
       bg = NA, 
       col = "black")
arrows(rep(0, CR.lv), rep(0, CR.lv), 
       CR.PCA$rotation[CR.vec, 1]/scal, 
       CR.PCA$rotation[CR.vec, 2]/scal, 
       length = 0.1, lwd = 3.5, angle = 20, 
       col = "black")
arrows(rep(0, CR.lv), rep(0, CR.lv), 
       CR.PCA$rotation[CR.vec, 1]/scal, 
       CR.PCA$rotation[CR.vec, 2]/scal, 
       length = 0.1, lwd = 2, angle = 20, 
       col = c("#B78560","#B7B7B7","#CC79A7","#43BA8F"))
text(CR.PCA$rotation[CR.vec, 1]/scal + jit * sign(CR.PCA$rotation[CR.vec, 1]), 
     CR.PCA$rotation[CR.vec, 2]/scal + jit * sign(CR.PCA$rotation[CR.vec, 2]), 
     labels = NULL,  cex = 0.5, pos = 2)
#labels = CR.vec,  cex = 0.5, pos = 2)
# Light gray background
#addTextLabels(CR.PCA.sites_names[, 3], 
#CR.PCA.sites_names[, 4], 
#CR.PCA.sites_names$Pop ,
#col.background=rgb(0,0,0,0.75),
#cex.label=0.7, col.label="white")
dev.off()

# Plot (map) predicted genetic turnover in geographic space
# *CR.PCA is remvoing NAs, so the CR.vector is shorter than it should and it's messing up positions. This code introduces back the NA rows in their original positions
Env.stack.2.grid$x <- 1:nrow(Env.stack.2.grid)
Env.stack.2.grid$nas <- complete.cases(Env.stack.2.grid)
CR.pcs <- as.data.frame(CR.PCA$x)
CR.pcs$x <- Env.stack.2.grid$x[Env.stack.2.grid$nas]
CR.pcs <- merge(CR.pcs, Env.stack.2.grid, by = "x", all = TRUE)
CR.pcs$r <- CR.pcs$PC1 + CR.pcs$PC2
CR.pcs$g <- -CR.pcs$PC2
CR.pcs$b <- CR.pcs$PC3 + CR.pcs$PC2 - CR.pcs$PC1
CR.pcs$r <- (CR.pcs$r - min(CR.pcs$r, na.rm = TRUE))/(max(CR.pcs$r, na.rm = TRUE) - min(CR.pcs$r, na.rm = TRUE)) * 255
CR.pcs$g <- (CR.pcs$g - min(CR.pcs$g, na.rm = TRUE))/(max(CR.pcs$g, na.rm = TRUE) - min(CR.pcs$g, na.rm = TRUE)) * 255
CR.pcs$b <- (CR.pcs$b - min(CR.pcs$b, na.rm = TRUE))/(max(CR.pcs$b, na.rm = TRUE) - min(CR.pcs$b, na.rm = TRUE)) * 255
CR.raster.r <- CR.raster.g <- CR.raster.b <- Env.stack.2[[1]]
values(CR.raster.r) <- CR.pcs$r
values(CR.raster.g) <- CR.pcs$g
values(CR.raster.b) <- CR.pcs$b
CR.rgbmap <- stack(CR.raster.r, CR.raster.g, CR.raster.b)

###Map spatial pattern of predicted genomic composition----
#Load geo coords
CR.coords <- read.csv("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/2_Chapter/2_2_Data/GeoSpatial/CR/CR_coords_Ch2.csv")

png("CR_Mapping_geographic_space_GF_BP_LF.png", width=800, height=1550, res=200)
plotRGB(CR.rgbmap)
points(CR.coords$lon, CR.coords$lat,pch = 21, lwd = 1.5, cex = 1, bg = "white")
dev.off()

CR.coords.shp=st_read("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/2_Chapter/2_2_Data/GeoSpatial/CR/CR_coords_Ch2.shp")

gnp <- st_read("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/1_Chapter/1_2_Data/GeoSpatial/Shapefiles/Grampians_W/Grampians_Wh_2.shp")

#hills <- raster("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/1_Chapter/1_2_Data/GeoSpatial/Climate_Raster/DEM_Grampians/hills.tif")

#dem <- raster("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/1_Chapter/1_2_Data/GeoSpatial/Climate_Raster/DEM_Grampians/GNP_dem.tif")

CR.coords.shp$Pop <- factor(
  CR.coords.shp$Pop,
  levels = unique(CR.coords.shp$Pop))

CR.rgbmap.spat <- rast(CR.rgbmap)

ggplot() +
  geom_spatraster_rgb(data = CR.rgbmap.spat) +
  geom_sf(data = gnp, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.5) +  
  geom_point(data = CR.coords.shp, aes(x = lon, y = lat, shape = Pop),
             size = 10, colour = "black", fill = "white",stroke = 1.5) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(24,
                                22,
                                24,
                                24,
                                24,
                                21,
                                24,
                                24,
                                23,
                                22,
                                23,
                                22,
                                23,
                                23,
                                9,
                                9,
                                23,
                                21,
                                21,
                                21,
                                22,
                                25,
                                23,
                                24,
                                24,
                                25,
                                25,
                                22)) +  
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) 

ggsave(file = "CR_GF_map.pdf", width = 12, height = 12, dpi = 300)

save(CR.rgbmap, file="CR.rgbmap.Rdata")
```

```{r Epacris impressa}
##load GF object (epa.BP.lfmm.GF) 
 
load("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/2_Chapter/2_3_DataAnalysis/gradientForest_Combined/BP_LF/EI/EI_GF_BP_LF.RData") 

#PCA of the transformed grid for plotting in RGB palette}

#The multi-dimensional biologiepa space can most effectively be represented by taking the principle components of the transformed grid and presenting the first two dimensions in a biplot.

# To allow straightforward ProEIustes superpositioning and generation of ensemble model, I only epaculate the first 8 PCs here. This is perfectly fine because I anyway only visualise the first 3 PCs. 
#Reference: https://github.com/hirzi/RhEA/blob/main/Gradient%20forest%20and%20glacial%20genomic%20offset/GradientForest_GlacialGenomicOffset.R#L361

# Transform variables by predicting with GF model
epa.vars <- names(importance(epa.BP.lfmm.GF))
EI.grid.pre <- predict(epa.BP.lfmm.GF, Env.stack.2.grid[complete.cases(Env.stack.2.grid), epa.vars]) # need to get rid of NA rows or an error pops up

# EI.PCA of the transformed grid for plotting in RGB palette
EI.PCA <- prcomp(na.omit(EI.grid.pre), center=TRUE, sepae.=FALSE)
save(EI.PCA, file="EI.PCA.RData")
EI.pcs.full.gf <- EI.PCA # Output for ensemble model generation
#Standard deviations (1, .., p=4):
#  [1] 0.021566050 0.012973156 0.008178401 0.006395288

#Rotation (n x k) = (4 x 4):
#             PC1        PC2        PC3        PC4
#pHc   -0.6879594  0.3988576 -0.5425818 -0.2706095
#TWI   -0.4040392 -0.8728784 -0.2142195  0.1701342
#BIO05 -0.5802777  0.1925890  0.6583791  0.4390036
#AVP    0.1635300  0.2047080 -0.4756594  0.8397027

summary(EI.PCA)
#Importance of components:
#                           PC1     PC2      PC3      PC4
#Standard deviation     0.02157 0.01297 0.008178 0.006395
#Proportion of Variance 0.62750 0.22707 0.090240 0.055180
#Cumulative Proportion  0.62750 0.85458 0.944820 1.000000

EI.a1 <- EI.PCA$x[, 1]
EI.a2 <- EI.PCA$x[, 2]
EI.a3 <- EI.PCA$x[, 3]
EI.r <- EI.a1 + EI.a2
EI.g <- -EI.a2
EI.b <- EI.a3 + EI.a2 - EI.a1
# Sepae colors
EI.r <- (EI.r - min(EI.r))/(max(EI.r) - min(EI.r)) * 255
EI.g <- (EI.g - min(EI.g))/(max(EI.g) - min(EI.g)) * 255
EI.b <- (EI.b - min(EI.b))/(max(EI.b) - min(EI.b)) * 255

# Plot predicted genetic turnover in genetic space (EI.PCA biplot)
EI.nvs <- dim(EI.PCA$rotation)[1]
EI.vec <- epa.vars
EI.lv <- length(EI.vec)
EI.vind <- rownames(EI.PCA$rotation) %in% EI.vec
sepa <- 20
EI.xrng <- range(EI.PCA$x[, 1], EI.PCA$rotation[, 1]/sepa) * 1.1
EI.yrng <- range(EI.PCA$x[, 2], EI.PCA$rotation[, 2]/sepa) * 1.1
EI.trns_site <- predict(epa.BP.lfmm.GF)
EI.PCA.sites <- predict(EI.PCA, EI.trns_site[, epa.vars])
jit <- 0.001

#write.csv(EI.PCA.sites, file="EI.PCA.sites.csv")
EI.PCA.sites_names <- read.csv("EI.PCA.sites.csv") 

#tiff("EI_Biplot_biologiepa_space_GF_BP_LF_2_labels.tiff", width=1500, height=1300, res=300)
#tiff("EI_Biplot_biologiepa_space_GF_BP_LF_2_vec.tiff", width=1500, height=1300, res=300)
tiff("EI_Biplot_biologiepa_space_GF_BP_LF_2.tiff", width=1500, height=1300, res=300)
par(mar = c(2, 2, 0.2, 0.5), mgp = c(0, 0, 0)) 
plot((EI.PCA$x[, 1:2]), xlim = EI.xrng, ylim = EI.yrng, xlab = "PC1 (63%)", ylab = "PC2 (23%)", 
     pch = ".", cex = 4, col = rgb(EI.r, EI.g, EI.b, max = 255), 
     asp = 1, cex.lab = 1.1, cex.axis = 1, frame.plot = FALSE, axes=FALSE, col.lab="white")
#rect(par("usr")[1], par("usr")[3],par("usr")[2], par("usr")[4])
#par(new = TRUE)
#plot((EI.PCA$x[, 1:2]), xlim = EI.xrng, ylim = EI.yrng, xlab = "PC1 (63%)", ylab = "PC2 (23%)", 
#     pch = ".", cex = 4, col = rgb(EI.r, EI.g, EI.b, max = 255), 
#     asp = 1, cex.lab = 2, cex.axis = 1)
#abline(h = 0, v = 0, col = "grey70", lwd = 1)
#axis(1, pos = 0, col = "grey70", labels = F, tck = 0) 
#axis(2, pos = 0, col = "grey70", labels = F, tck = 0)
points(EI.PCA.sites_names[, 3:4],  pch = c(25,
                                           24,
                                           22,
                                           24,
                                           24,
                                           24,
                                           21,
                                           24,
                                           24,
                                           23,
                                           22,
                                           23,
                                           23,
                                           9,
                                           23,
                                           21,
                                           21,
                                           21,
                                           22,
                                           25,
                                           23,
                                           24,
                                           24,
                                           25,
                                           25,
                                           22,
                                           22), lwd = 0.7, cex = 1, 
       bg = NA, 
       col = "black")
arrows(rep(0, EI.lv), rep(0, EI.lv), 
       EI.PCA$rotation[EI.vec, 1]/sepa, 
       EI.PCA$rotation[EI.vec, 2]/sepa, 
       length = 0.1, lwd = 3.5, angle = 20, 
       col = "black")
arrows(rep(0, EI.lv), rep(0, EI.lv), 
       EI.PCA$rotation[EI.vec, 1]/sepa, 
       EI.PCA$rotation[EI.vec, 2]/sepa, 
       length = 0.1, lwd = 2, angle = 20, 
       col = c("#43BA8F","#B78560","#CC79A7","#B7B7B7"))
text(EI.PCA$rotation[EI.vec, 1]/sepa + jit * sign(EI.PCA$rotation[EI.vec, 1]), 
     EI.PCA$rotation[EI.vec, 2]/sepa + jit * sign(EI.PCA$rotation[EI.vec, 2]), 
     labels = NULL,  cex = 0.5, pos = 2)
#labels = EI.vec,  cex = 0.5, pos = 2)
# Light gray background
#addTextLabels(EI.PCA.sites_names[, 3], 
#EI.PCA.sites_names[, 4], 
#EI.PCA.sites_names$Pop ,
#col.background=rgb(0,0,0,0.75),
#cex.label=0.7, col.label="white")
dev.off()

# Plot (map) predicted genetic turnover in geographic space
# *EI.PCA is remvoing NAs, so the EI.vector is shorter than it should and it's messing up positions. This code introduces back the NA rows in their original positions
Env.stack.2.grid$x <- 1:nrow(Env.stack.2.grid)
Env.stack.2.grid$nas <- complete.cases(Env.stack.2.grid)
EI.pcs <- as.data.frame(EI.PCA$x)
EI.pcs$x <- Env.stack.2.grid$x[Env.stack.2.grid$nas]
EI.pcs <- merge(EI.pcs, Env.stack.2.grid, by = "x", all = TRUE)
EI.pcs$r <- EI.pcs$PC1 + EI.pcs$PC2
EI.pcs$g <- -EI.pcs$PC2
EI.pcs$b <- EI.pcs$PC3 + EI.pcs$PC2 - EI.pcs$PC1
EI.pcs$r <- (EI.pcs$r - min(EI.pcs$r, na.rm = TRUE))/(max(EI.pcs$r, na.rm = TRUE) - min(EI.pcs$r, na.rm = TRUE)) * 255
EI.pcs$g <- (EI.pcs$g - min(EI.pcs$g, na.rm = TRUE))/(max(EI.pcs$g, na.rm = TRUE) - min(EI.pcs$g, na.rm = TRUE)) * 255
EI.pcs$b <- (EI.pcs$b - min(EI.pcs$b, na.rm = TRUE))/(max(EI.pcs$b, na.rm = TRUE) - min(EI.pcs$b, na.rm = TRUE)) * 255
EI.raster.r <- EI.raster.g <- EI.raster.b <- Env.stack.2[[1]]
values(EI.raster.r) <- EI.pcs$r
values(EI.raster.g) <- EI.pcs$g
values(EI.raster.b) <- EI.pcs$b
EI.rgbmap <- stack(EI.raster.r, EI.raster.g, EI.raster.b)

###Map spatial pattern of predicted genomic composition----
#Load geo coords
EI.coords <- read.csv("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/2_Chapter/2_2_Data/GeoSpatial/EI/EI_coords_Ch2.csv")

png("EI_Mapping_geographic_space_GF_BP_LF.png", width=800, height=1550, res=200)
plotRGB(EI.rgbmap)
points(EI.coords$lon, EI.coords$lat,pch = 21, lwd = 1.5, cex = 1, bg = "white")
dev.off()

EI.coords.shp=st_read("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/2_Chapter/2_2_Data/GeoSpatial/EI/EI_coords_Ch2.shp")

gnp <- st_read("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/1_Chapter/1_2_Data/GeoSpatial/Shapefiles/Grampians_W/Grampians_Wh_2.shp")

#hills <- raster("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/1_Chapter/1_2_Data/GeoSpatial/Climate_Raster/DEM_Grampians/hills.tif")

#dem <- raster("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/1_Chapter/1_2_Data/GeoSpatial/Climate_Raster/DEM_Grampians/GNP_dem.tif")

EI.coords.shp$Pop <- factor(
  EI.coords.shp$Pop,
  levels = unique(EI.coords.shp$Pop))

EI.rgbmap.spat <- rast(EI.rgbmap)

ggplot() +
  geom_spatraster_rgb(data = EI.rgbmap.spat) +
  geom_sf(data = gnp, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.5) +  
  geom_point(data = EI.coords.shp, aes(x = lon, y = lat, shape = Pop),
             size = 10, colour = "black", fill = "white",stroke = 1.5) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(25,
                                24,
                                22,
                                24,
                                24,
                                24,
                                21,
                                24,
                                24,
                                23,
                                22,
                                23,
                                23,
                                9,
                                23,
                                21,
                                21,
                                21,
                                22,
                                25,
                                23,
                                24,
                                24,
                                25,
                                25,
                                22,
                                22)) +  
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) 

ggsave(file = "EI_GF_map.pdf", width = 12, height = 12, dpi = 300)

save(EI.rgbmap, file="EI.rgbmap.Rdata")
```

```{r Eucalyptus obliqua}
##load GF object (gre.BP.lfmm.GF) 

load("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/2_Chapter/2_3_DataAnalysis/gradientForest_Combined/BP_LF/GA/GA_GF_BP_LF.RData") 

#PCA of the transformed grid for plotting in RGB palette}

#The multi-dimensional biologigre space can most effectively be represented by taking the principle components of the transformed grid and presenting the first two dimensions in a biplot.

# To allow straightforward ProGAustes superpositioning and generation of ensemble model, I only greculate the first 8 PCs here. This is perfectly fine because I anyway only visualise the first 3 PCs. 
#Reference: https://github.com/hirzi/RhEA/blob/main/Gradient%20forest%20and%20glacial%20genomic%20offset/GradientForest_GlacialGenomicOffset.R#L361

# Transform variables by predicting with GF model
gre.vars <- names(importance(gre.BP.lfmm.GF))
GA.grid.pre <- predict(gre.BP.lfmm.GF, Env.stack.2.grid[complete.cases(Env.stack.2.grid), gre.vars]) # need to get rid of NA rows or an error pops up

# GA.PCA of the transformed grid for plotting in RGB palette
GA.PCA <- prcomp(na.omit(GA.grid.pre), center=TRUE, scale.=FALSE)
save(GA.PCA, file="GA.PCA.RData")
GA.pcs.full.gf <- GA.PCA # Output for ensemble model generation
#Standard deviations (1, .., p=4):
#  [1] 0.014744988 0.010031450 0.005660944 0.004007094

#Rotation (n x k) = (4 x 4):
#             PC1         PC2         PC3         PC4
#AVP    0.8420279 -0.50386179 -0.16878209 -0.09287060
#TWI   -0.2410700 -0.08576805 -0.96606432  0.03533836
#pHc   -0.3742612 -0.77379302  0.17959229  0.47845538
#BIO05 -0.3046356 -0.37418111  0.07732362 -0.87247160

summary(GA.PCA)
#Importance of components:
#                           PC1     PC2      PC3      PC4
#Standard deviation     0.01474 0.01003 0.005661 0.004007
#Proportion of Variance 0.59379 0.27483 0.087520 0.043850
#Cumulative Proportion  0.59379 0.86862 0.956150 1.000000

GA.a1 <- GA.PCA$x[, 1]
GA.a2 <- GA.PCA$x[, 2]
GA.a3 <- GA.PCA$x[, 3]
GA.r <- GA.a1 + GA.a2
GA.g <- -GA.a2
GA.b <- GA.a3 + GA.a2 - GA.a1
# scale colors
GA.r <- (GA.r - min(GA.r))/(max(GA.r) - min(GA.r)) * 255
GA.g <- (GA.g - min(GA.g))/(max(GA.g) - min(GA.g)) * 255
GA.b <- (GA.b - min(GA.b))/(max(GA.b) - min(GA.b)) * 255

# Plot predicted genetic turnover in genetic space (GA.PCA biplot)
GA.nvs <- dim(GA.PCA$rotation)[1]
GA.vec <- gre.vars
GA.lv <- length(GA.vec)
GA.vind <- rownames(GA.PCA$rotation) %in% GA.vec
scal <- 20
GA.xrng <- range(GA.PCA$x[, 1], GA.PCA$rotation[, 1]/scal) * 1.1
GA.yrng <- range(GA.PCA$x[, 2], GA.PCA$rotation[, 2]/scal) * 1.1
GA.trns_site <- predict(gre.BP.lfmm.GF)
GA.PCA.sites <- predict(GA.PCA, GA.trns_site[, gre.vars])
jit <- 0.001

#write.csv(GA.PCA.sites, file="GA.PCA.sites.csv")
GA.PCA.sites_names <- read.csv("GA.PCA.sites.csv") 

#tiff("GA_Biplot_biologigre_space_GF_BP_LF_2_labels.tiff", width=1500, height=1300, res=300)
#tiff("GA_Biplot_biologigre_space_GF_BP_LF_2_vec.tiff", width=1500, height=1300, res=300)
tiff("GA_Biplot_biologigre_space_GF_BP_LF_2.tiff", width=1500, height=1300, res=300)
par(mar = c(2, 2, 0.2, 0.5), mgp = c(0, 0, 0)) 
plot((GA.PCA$x[, 1:2]), xlim = GA.xrng, ylim = GA.yrng, xlab = "PC1 (59%)", ylab = "PC2 (27%)", 
     pch = ".", cex = 4, col = rgb(GA.r, GA.g, GA.b, max = 255), 
     asp = 1, cex.lab = 1.1, cex.axis = 1, frame.plot = FALSE, axes=FALSE, col.lab="white")
#rect(par("usr")[1], par("usr")[3],par("usr")[2], par("usr")[4])
#par(new = TRUE)
#plot((GA.PCA$x[, 1:2]), xlim = GA.xrng, ylim = GA.yrng, xlab = "PC1 (59%)", ylab = "PC2 (27%)", 
#     pch = ".", cex = 4, col = rgb(GA.r, GA.g, GA.b, max = 255), 
#     asp = 1, cex.lab = 2, cex.axis = 1)
#abline(h = 0, v = 0, col = "grey70", lwd = 1)
#axis(1, pos = 0, col = "grey70", labels = F, tck = 0) 
#axis(2, pos = 0, col = "grey70", labels = F, tck = 0)
points(GA.PCA.sites_names[, 3:4],  pch = c(25,
                                           24,
                                           22,
                                           24,
                                           24,
                                           21,
                                           24,
                                           24,
                                           23,
                                           22,
                                           23,
                                           22,
                                           23,
                                           23,
                                           9,
                                           9,
                                           23,
                                           21,
                                           21,
                                           21,
                                           22,
                                           25,
                                           23,
                                           24,
                                           24,
                                           25,
                                           25,
                                           22,
                                           22), lwd = 0.7, cex =1, 
       bg = NA, 
       col = "black")
arrows(rep(0, GA.lv), rep(0, GA.lv), 
       GA.PCA$rotation[GA.vec, 1]/scal, 
       GA.PCA$rotation[GA.vec, 2]/scal, 
       length = 0.1, lwd = 3.5, angle = 20, 
       col = "black")
arrows(rep(0, GA.lv), rep(0, GA.lv), 
       GA.PCA$rotation[GA.vec, 1]/scal, 
       GA.PCA$rotation[GA.vec, 2]/scal, 
       length = 0.1, lwd = 2, angle = 20, 
       col = c("#B7B7B7","#B78560","#43BA8F","#CC79A7"))
text(GA.PCA$rotation[GA.vec, 1]/scal + jit * sign(GA.PCA$rotation[GA.vec, 1]), 
     GA.PCA$rotation[GA.vec, 2]/scal + jit * sign(GA.PCA$rotation[GA.vec, 2]), 
     labels = NULL,  cex = 0.5, pos = 2)
#labels = GA.vec,  cex = 0.5, pos = 2)
# Light gray background
#addTextLabels(GA.PCA.sites_names[, 3], 
#GA.PCA.sites_names[, 4], 
#GA.PCA.sites_names$Pop ,
#col.background=rgb(0,0,0,0.75),
#cex.label=0.7, col.label="white")
dev.off()

# Plot (map) predicted genetic turnover in geographic space
# *GA.PCA is remvoing NAs, so the GA.vector is shorter than it should and it's messing up positions. This code introduces back the NA rows in their original positions
Env.stack.2.grid$x <- 1:nrow(Env.stack.2.grid)
Env.stack.2.grid$nas <- complete.cases(Env.stack.2.grid)
GA.pcs <- as.data.frame(GA.PCA$x)
GA.pcs$x <- Env.stack.2.grid$x[Env.stack.2.grid$nas]
GA.pcs <- merge(GA.pcs, Env.stack.2.grid, by = "x", all = TRUE)
GA.pcs$r <- GA.pcs$PC1 + GA.pcs$PC2
GA.pcs$g <- -GA.pcs$PC2
GA.pcs$b <- GA.pcs$PC3 + GA.pcs$PC2 - GA.pcs$PC1
GA.pcs$r <- (GA.pcs$r - min(GA.pcs$r, na.rm = TRUE))/(max(GA.pcs$r, na.rm = TRUE) - min(GA.pcs$r, na.rm = TRUE)) * 255
GA.pcs$g <- (GA.pcs$g - min(GA.pcs$g, na.rm = TRUE))/(max(GA.pcs$g, na.rm = TRUE) - min(GA.pcs$g, na.rm = TRUE)) * 255
GA.pcs$b <- (GA.pcs$b - min(GA.pcs$b, na.rm = TRUE))/(max(GA.pcs$b, na.rm = TRUE) - min(GA.pcs$b, na.rm = TRUE)) * 255
GA.raster.r <- GA.raster.g <- GA.raster.b <- Env.stack.2[[1]]
values(GA.raster.r) <- GA.pcs$r
values(GA.raster.g) <- GA.pcs$g
values(GA.raster.b) <- GA.pcs$b
GA.rgbmap <- stack(GA.raster.r, GA.raster.g, GA.raster.b)

###Map spatial pattern of predicted genomic composition----
#Load geo coords
GA.coords <- read.csv("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/2_Chapter/2_2_Data/GeoSpatial/GA/GA_coords_Ch2.csv")

png("GA_Mapping_geographic_space_GF_BP_LF.png", width=800, height=1550, res=200)
plotRGB(GA.rgbmap)
points(GA.coords$lon, GA.coords$lat,pch = 21, lwd = 1.5, cex = 1, bg = "white")
dev.off()

GA.coords.shp=st_read("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/2_Chapter/2_2_Data/GeoSpatial/GA/GA_coords_Ch2.shp")

gnp <- st_read("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/1_Chapter/1_2_Data/GeoSpatial/Shapefiles/Grampians_W/Grampians_Wh_2.shp")

#hills <- raster("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/1_Chapter/1_2_Data/GeoSpatial/Climate_Raster/DEM_Grampians/hills.tif")

#dem <- raster("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/1_Chapter/1_2_Data/GeoSpatial/Climate_Raster/DEM_Grampians/GNP_dem.tif")

GA.coords.shp$Pop <- factor(
  GA.coords.shp$Pop,
  levels = unique(GA.coords.shp$Pop)
)

GA.rgbmap.spat <- rast(GA.rgbmap)

ggplot() +
  geom_spatraster_rgb(data = GA.rgbmap.spat) +
  geom_sf(data = gnp, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.5) +  
  geom_point(data = GA.coords.shp, aes(x = lon, y = lat, shape = Pop),
             size = 10, colour = "black", fill = "white",stroke = 1.5) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(25,
                                24,
                                22,
                                24,
                                24,
                                21,
                                24,
                                24,
                                23,
                                22,
                                23,
                                22,
                                23,
                                23,
                                9,
                                9,
                                23,
                                21,
                                21,
                                21,
                                22,
                                25,
                                23,
                                24,
                                24,
                                25,
                                25,
                                22,
                                22)) +  
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) 

ggsave(file = "GA_GF_map.pdf", width = 12, height = 12, dpi = 300)

save(GA.rgbmap, file="GA.rgbmap.Rdata") 
```

```{r Grevillea aquifolium}
##load GF object (gre.BP.lfmm.GF.vif) 

load("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/2_Chapter/2_3_DataAnalysis/gradientForest_Combined/BP_LF/GA/vif/GA_GF_BP_LF_VIF.RData") 

#PCA of the transformed grid for plotting in RGB palette}

#The multi-dimensional biologigre space can most effectively be represented by taking the principle components of the transformed grid and presenting the first two dimensions in a biplot.

# To allow straightforward ProGAustes superpositioning and generation of ensemble model, I only greculate the first 8 PCs here. This is perfectly fine because I anyway only visualise the first 3 PCs. 
#Reference: https://github.com/hirzi/RhEA/blob/main/Gradient%20forest%20and%20glacial%20genomic%20offset/GradientForest_GlacialGenomicOffset.R#L361

# Transform variables by predicting with GF model
gre.vars.vif <- names(importance(gre.BP.lfmm.GF.vif))
GA.grid.pre.vif <- predict(gre.BP.lfmm.GF.vif, Env.stack.2.grid[complete.cases(Env.stack.2.grid), gre.vars.vif]) # need to get rid of NA rows or an error pops up

# GA.PCA.vif of the transformed grid for plotting in RGB palette
GA.PCA.vif <- prcomp(na.omit(GA.grid.pre.vif), center=TRUE, scale.=FALSE)
save(GA.PCA.vif, file="GA.PCA.vif.RData")
GA.pcs.vif.full.gf.vif <- GA.PCA.vif # Output for ensemble model generation
#Standard deviations (1, .., p=4):
#  [1] 0.014744988 0.010031450 0.005660944 0.004007094

#Rotation (n x k) = (4 x 4):
#             PC1         PC2         PC3         PC4
#AVP    0.8420279 -0.50386179 -0.16878209 -0.09287060
#TWI   -0.2410700 -0.08576805 -0.96606432  0.03533836
#pHc   -0.3742612 -0.77379302  0.17959229  0.47845538
#BIO05 -0.3046356 -0.37418111  0.07732362 -0.87247160

summary(GA.PCA.vif)
#Importance of components:
#                           PC1     PC2      PC3      PC4
#Standard deviation     0.01474 0.01003 0.005661 0.004007
#Proportion of Variance 0.59379 0.27483 0.087520 0.043850
#Cumulative Proportion  0.59379 0.86862 0.956150 1.000000

GA.a1.vif <- GA.PCA.vif$x[, 1]
GA.a2.vif <- GA.PCA.vif$x[, 2]
GA.a3.vif <- GA.PCA.vif$x[, 3]
GA.r.vif <- GA.a1.vif + GA.a2.vif
GA.g.vif <- -GA.a2.vif
GA.b.vif <- GA.a3.vif + GA.a2.vif - GA.a1.vif
# scale colors
GA.r.vif <- (GA.r.vif - min(GA.r.vif))/(max(GA.r.vif) - min(GA.r.vif)) * 255
GA.g.vif <- (GA.g.vif - min(GA.g.vif))/(max(GA.g.vif) - min(GA.g.vif)) * 255
GA.b.vif <- (GA.b.vif - min(GA.b.vif))/(max(GA.b.vif) - min(GA.b.vif)) * 255

# Plot predicted genetic turnover in genetic space (GA.PCA.vif biplot)
GA.nvs.vif <- dim(GA.PCA.vif$rotation)[1]
GA.vec.vif <- gre.vars.vif
GA.lv.vif <- length(GA.vec.vif)
GA.vind.vif <- rownames(GA.PCA.vif$rotation) %in% GA.vec.vif
scal <- 20
GA.xrng.vif <- range(GA.PCA.vif$x[, 1], GA.PCA.vif$rotation[, 1]/scal) * 1.1
GA.yrng.vif <- range(GA.PCA.vif$x[, 2], GA.PCA.vif$rotation[, 2]/scal) * 1.1
GA.trns_site.vif <- predict(gre.BP.lfmm.GF.vif)
GA.PCA.vif.sites <- predict(GA.PCA.vif, GA.trns_site.vif[, gre.vars.vif])
jit <- 0.001

#write.csv(GA.PCA.vif.sites, file="GA.PCA.vif.sites.csv")
GA.PCA.vif.sites_names <- read.csv("GA.PCA.vif.sites.csv") 

#tiff("GA_Biplot_biologigre_space_GF_BP_LF_2_labels_vif.tiff", width=1500, height=1300, res=300)
#tiff("GA_Biplot_biologigre_space_GF_BP_LF_2_vec_vif.tiff", width=1500, height=1300, res=300)
tiff("GA_Biplot_biologigre_space_GF_BP_LF_2_vif.tiff", width=1500, height=1300, res=300)
par(mar = c(2, 2, 0.2, 0.5), mgp = c(0, 0, 0)) 
plot((GA.PCA.vif$x[, 1:2]), xlim = GA.xrng.vif, ylim = GA.yrng.vif, xlab = "PC1 (59%)", ylab = "PC2 (27%)", 
     pch = ".", cex = 4, col = rgb(GA.r.vif, GA.g.vif, GA.b.vif, max = 255), 
     asp = 1, cex.lab = 1.1, cex.axis = 1, frame.plot = FALSE, axes=FALSE, col.lab="white")
#rect(par("usr")[1], par("usr")[3],par("usr")[2], par("usr")[4])
#par(new = TRUE)
#plot((GA.PCA.vif$x[, 1:2]), xlim = GA.xrng.vif, ylim = GA.yrng.vif, xlab = "PC1 (59%)", ylab = "PC2 (27%)", 
#     pch = ".", cex = 4, col = rgb(GA.r, GA.g, GA.b, max = 255), 
#     asp = 1, cex.lab = 2, cex.axis = 1)
#abline(h = 0, v = 0, col = "grey70", lwd = 1)
#axis(1, pos = 0, col = "grey70", labels = F, tck = 0) 
#axis(2, pos = 0, col = "grey70", labels = F, tck = 0)
points(GA.PCA.vif.sites_names[, 3:4],  pch = c(25,
                                           24,
                                           22,
                                           24,
                                           24,
                                           21,
                                           24,
                                           24,
                                           23,
                                           22,
                                           23,
                                           22,
                                           23,
                                           23,
                                           9,
                                           9,
                                           23,
                                           21,
                                           21,
                                           21,
                                           22,
                                           25,
                                           23,
                                           24,
                                           24,
                                           25,
                                           25,
                                           22,
                                           22), lwd = 0.7, cex =1, 
       bg = NA, 
       col = "black")
arrows(rep(0, GA.lv.vif), rep(0, GA.lv.vif), 
       GA.PCA.vif$rotation[GA.vec.vif, 1]/scal, 
       GA.PCA.vif$rotation[GA.vec.vif, 2]/scal, 
       length = 0.1, lwd = 3.5, angle = 20, 
       col = "black")
arrows(rep(0, GA.lv.vif), rep(0, GA.lv.vif), 
       GA.PCA.vif$rotation[GA.vec.vif, 1]/scal, 
       GA.PCA.vif$rotation[GA.vec.vif, 2]/scal, 
       length = 0.1, lwd = 2, angle = 20, 
       col = c("#B7B7B7","#B78560","#43BA8F","#CC79A7"))
text(GA.PCA.vif$rotation[GA.vec.vif, 1]/scal + jit * sign(GA.PCA.vif$rotation[GA.vec.vif, 1]), 
     GA.PCA.vif$rotation[GA.vec.vif, 2]/scal + jit * sign(GA.PCA.vif$rotation[GA.vec.vif, 2]), 
     labels = NULL,  cex = 0.5, pos = 2)
#labels = GA.vec.vif,  cex = 0.5, pos = 2)
# Light gray background
#addTextLabels(GA.PCA.vif.sites_names[, 3], 
#GA.PCA.vif.sites_names[, 4], 
#GA.PCA.vif.sites_names$Pop ,
#col.background=rgb(0,0,0,0.75),
#cex.label=0.7, col.label="white")
dev.off()

# Plot (map) predicted genetic turnover in geographic space
# *GA.PCA.vif is remvoing NAs, so the GA.vec.viftor is shorter than it should and it's messing up positions. This code introduces back the NA rows in their original positions
Env.stack.2.grid$x <- 1:nrow(Env.stack.2.grid)
Env.stack.2.grid$nas <- complete.cases(Env.stack.2.grid)
GA.pcs.vif <- as.data.frame(GA.PCA.vif$x)
GA.pcs.vif$x <- Env.stack.2.grid$x[Env.stack.2.grid$nas]
GA.pcs.vif <- merge(GA.pcs.vif, Env.stack.2.grid, by = "x", all = TRUE)
GA.pcs.vif$r <- GA.pcs.vif$PC1 + GA.pcs.vif$PC2
GA.pcs.vif$g <- -GA.pcs.vif$PC2
GA.pcs.vif$b <- GA.pcs.vif$PC3 + GA.pcs.vif$PC2 - GA.pcs.vif$PC1
GA.pcs.vif$r <- (GA.pcs.vif$r - min(GA.pcs.vif$r, na.rm = TRUE))/(max(GA.pcs.vif$r, na.rm = TRUE) - min(GA.pcs.vif$r, na.rm = TRUE)) * 255
GA.pcs.vif$g <- (GA.pcs.vif$g - min(GA.pcs.vif$g, na.rm = TRUE))/(max(GA.pcs.vif$g, na.rm = TRUE) - min(GA.pcs.vif$g, na.rm = TRUE)) * 255
GA.pcs.vif$b <- (GA.pcs.vif$b - min(GA.pcs.vif$b, na.rm = TRUE))/(max(GA.pcs.vif$b, na.rm = TRUE) - min(GA.pcs.vif$b, na.rm = TRUE)) * 255
GA.raster.r.vif <- GA.raster.g.vif <- GA.raster.b.vif <- Env.stack.2[[1]]
values(GA.raster.r.vif) <- GA.pcs.vif$r
values(GA.raster.g.vif) <- GA.pcs.vif$g
values(GA.raster.b.vif) <- GA.pcs.vif$b
GA.rgbmap.vif <- stack(GA.raster.r.vif, GA.raster.g.vif, GA.raster.b.vif)

###Map spatial pattern of predicted genomic composition----
#Load geo coords
GA.coords <- read.csv("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/2_Chapter/2_2_Data/GeoSpatial/GA/GA_coords_Ch2.csv")

png("GA_Mapping_geographic_space_GF_BP_LF_vif.png", width=800, height=1550, res=200)
plotRGB(GA.rgbmap.vif)
points(GA.coords$lon, GA.coords$lat,pch = 21, lwd = 1.5, cex = 1, bg = "white")
dev.off()

GA.coords.shp=st_read("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/2_Chapter/2_2_Data/GeoSpatial/GA/GA_coords_Ch2.shp")

gnp <- st_read("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/1_Chapter/1_2_Data/GeoSpatial/Shapefiles/Grampians_W/Grampians_Wh_2.shp")

#hills <- raster("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/1_Chapter/1_2_Data/GeoSpatial/Climate_Raster/DEM_Grampians/hills.tif")

#dem <- raster("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/1_Chapter/1_2_Data/GeoSpatial/Climate_Raster/DEM_Grampians/GNP_dem.tif")

GA.coords.shp$Pop <- factor(
  GA.coords.shp$Pop,
  levels = unique(GA.coords.shp$Pop)
)

GA.rgbmap.vif.spat <- rast(GA.rgbmap.vif)

ggplot() +
  geom_spatraster_rgb(data = GA.rgbmap.vif.spat) +
  geom_sf(data = gnp, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.5) +  
  geom_point(data = GA.coords.shp, aes(x = lon, y = lat, shape = Pop),
             size = 10, colour = "black", fill = "white",stroke = 1.5) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(25,
                                24,
                                22,
                                24,
                                24,
                                21,
                                24,
                                24,
                                23,
                                22,
                                23,
                                22,
                                23,
                                23,
                                9,
                                9,
                                23,
                                21,
                                21,
                                21,
                                22,
                                25,
                                23,
                                24,
                                24,
                                25,
                                25,
                                22,
                                22)) +  
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) 

ggsave(file = "GA_GF_map_vif.pdf", width = 12, height = 12, dpi = 300)

save(GA.rgbmap.vif, file="GA.rgbmap.vif.Rdata")
```

```{r Xanthorrhoea australis}
##load GF object (xan.BP.lfmm.GF) 

load("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/2_Chapter/2_3_DataAnalysis/gradientForest_Combined/BP_LF/XA/XA_GF_BP_LF.RData") 

#PCA of the transformed grid for plotting in RGB palette}

#The multi-dimensional biologixan space can most effectively be represented by taking the principle components of the transformed grid and presenting the first two dimensions in a biplot.

# To allow straightforward ProXAustes superpositioning and generation of ensemble model, I only xanculate the first 8 PCs here. This is perfectly fine because I anyway only visualise the first 3 PCs. 
#Reference: https://github.com/hirzi/RhEA/blob/main/Gradient%20forest%20and%20glacial%20genomic%20offset/GradientForest_GlacialGenomicOffset.R#L361

# Transform variables by predicting with GF model
xan.vars <- names(importance(xan.BP.lfmm.GF))
XA.grid.pre <- predict(xan.BP.lfmm.GF, Env.stack.2.grid[complete.cases(Env.stack.2.grid), xan.vars]) # need to get rid of NA rows or an error pops up

# XA.PCA of the transformed grid for plotting in RGB palette
XA.PCA <- prcomp(na.omit(XA.grid.pre), center=TRUE, scale.=FALSE)
save(XA.PCA, file="XA.PCA.RData")
XA.pcs.full.gf <- XA.PCA # Output for ensemble model generation
#Standard deviations (1, .., p=4):
#  [1] 0.018842402 0.011470832 0.010728110 0.005109098

#Rotation (n x k) = (4 x 4):
#             PC1        PC2         PC3          PC4
#TWI    0.6133954 -0.6145383 -0.49601909  0.007333358
#BIO05  0.3822248  0.4118318 -0.02533762  0.826835411
#AVP   -0.5448735  0.1144524 -0.81309769  0.169957747
#pHc    0.4251624  0.6630520 -0.30363666 -0.536100540

summary(XA.PCA)
#Importance of components:
#                           PC1     PC2     PC3      PC4
#Standard deviation     0.01884 0.01147 0.01073 0.005109
#Proportion of Variance 0.56551 0.20959 0.18332 0.041580
#Cumulative Proportion  0.56551 0.77510 0.95842 1.000000

XA.a1 <- XA.PCA$x[, 1]
XA.a2 <- XA.PCA$x[, 2]
XA.a3 <- XA.PCA$x[, 3]
XA.r <- XA.a1 + XA.a2
XA.g <- -XA.a2
XA.b <- XA.a3 + XA.a2 - XA.a1
# scale colors
XA.r <- (XA.r - min(XA.r))/(max(XA.r) - min(XA.r)) * 255
XA.g <- (XA.g - min(XA.g))/(max(XA.g) - min(XA.g)) * 255
XA.b <- (XA.b - min(XA.b))/(max(XA.b) - min(XA.b)) * 255

# Plot predicted genetic turnover in genetic space (XA.PCA biplot)
XA.nvs <- dim(XA.PCA$rotation)[1]
XA.vec <- xan.vars
XA.lv <- length(XA.vec)
XA.vind <- rownames(XA.PCA$rotation) %in% XA.vec
scal <- 20
XA.xrng <- range(XA.PCA$x[, 1], XA.PCA$rotation[, 1]/scal) * 1.1
XA.yrng <- range(XA.PCA$x[, 2], XA.PCA$rotation[, 2]/scal) * 1.1
XA.trns_site <- predict(xan.BP.lfmm.GF)
XA.PCA.sites <- predict(XA.PCA, XA.trns_site[, xan.vars])
jit <- 0.001

#write.csv(XA.PCA.sites, file="XA.PCA.sites.csv")
XA.PCA.sites_names <- read.csv("XA.PCA.sites.csv") 

#tiff("XA_Biplot_biologixan_space_GF_BP_LF_2_labels.tiff", width=1500, height=1300, res=300)
#tiff("XA_Biplot_biologixan_space_GF_BP_LF_2_vec.tiff", width=1500, height=1300, res=300)
tiff("XA_Biplot_biologixan_space_GF_BP_LF_2.tiff", width=1500, height=1300, res=300)
par(mar = c(2, 2, 0.2, 0.5), mgp = c(0, 0, 0)) 
plot((XA.PCA$x[, 1:2]), xlim = XA.xrng, ylim = XA.yrng, xlab = "PC1 (57%)", ylab = "PC2 (21%)", 
     pch = ".", cex = 4, col = rgb(XA.r, XA.g, XA.b, max = 255), 
     asp = 1, cex.lab = 1.1, cex.axis = 1, frame.plot = FALSE, axes=FALSE, col.lab="white")
#rect(par("usr")[1], par("usr")[3],par("usr")[2], par("usr")[4])
#par(new = TRUE)
#plot((XA.PCA$x[, 1:2]), xlim = XA.xrng, ylim = XA.yrng, xlab = "PC1 (57%)", ylab = "PC2 (21%)", 
#     pch = ".", cex = 4, col = rgb(XA.r, XA.g, XA.b, max = 255), 
#     asp = 1, cex.lab = 2, cex.axis = 1)
#abline(h = 0, v = 0, col = "grey70", lwd = 1)
#axis(1, pos = 0, col = "grey70", labels = F, tck = 0) 
#axis(2, pos = 0, col = "grey70", labels = F, tck = 0)
points(XA.PCA.sites_names[, 3:4],  pch = c(25,
                                           24,
                                           22,
                                           24,
                                           24,
                                           24,
                                           21,
                                           24,
                                           24,
                                           23,
                                           22,
                                           23,
                                           22,
                                           23,
                                           23,
                                           9,
                                           9,
                                           23,
                                           21,
                                           21,
                                           21,
                                           22,
                                           25,
                                           23,
                                           24,
                                           24,
                                           25,
                                           25,
                                           22,
                                           22), lwd = 0.7, cex = 1, 
       bg = NA, 
       col = "black")
arrows(rep(0, XA.lv), rep(0, XA.lv), 
       XA.PCA$rotation[XA.vec, 1]/scal, 
       XA.PCA$rotation[XA.vec, 2]/scal, 
       length = 0.1, lwd = 3.5, angle = 20, 
       col = "black")
arrows(rep(0, XA.lv), rep(0, XA.lv), 
       XA.PCA$rotation[XA.vec, 1]/scal, 
       XA.PCA$rotation[XA.vec, 2]/scal, 
       length = 0.1, lwd = 2, angle = 20, 
       col = c("#B78560","#CC79A7","#B7B7B7","#43BA8F"))
text(XA.PCA$rotation[XA.vec, 1]/scal + jit * sign(XA.PCA$rotation[XA.vec, 1]), 
     XA.PCA$rotation[XA.vec, 2]/scal + jit * sign(XA.PCA$rotation[XA.vec, 2]), 
     labels = NULL,  cex = 0.5, pos = 2)
#labels = XA.vec,  cex = 0.5, pos = 2)
# Light gray background
#addTextLabels(XA.PCA.sites_names[, 3], 
#XA.PCA.sites_names[, 4], 
#XA.PCA.sites_names$Pop ,
#col.background=rgb(0,0,0,0.75),
#cex.label=0.7, col.label="white")
dev.off()

# Plot (map) predicted genetic turnover in geographic space
# *XA.PCA is remvoing NAs, so the XA.vector is shorter than it should and it's messing up positions. This code introduces back the NA rows in their original positions
Env.stack.2.grid$x <- 1:nrow(Env.stack.2.grid)
Env.stack.2.grid$nas <- complete.cases(Env.stack.2.grid)
XA.pcs <- as.data.frame(XA.PCA$x)
XA.pcs$x <- Env.stack.2.grid$x[Env.stack.2.grid$nas]
XA.pcs <- merge(XA.pcs, Env.stack.2.grid, by = "x", all = TRUE)
XA.pcs$r <- XA.pcs$PC1 + XA.pcs$PC2
XA.pcs$g <- -XA.pcs$PC2
XA.pcs$b <- XA.pcs$PC3 + XA.pcs$PC2 - XA.pcs$PC1
XA.pcs$r <- (XA.pcs$r - min(XA.pcs$r, na.rm = TRUE))/(max(XA.pcs$r, na.rm = TRUE) - min(XA.pcs$r, na.rm = TRUE)) * 255
XA.pcs$g <- (XA.pcs$g - min(XA.pcs$g, na.rm = TRUE))/(max(XA.pcs$g, na.rm = TRUE) - min(XA.pcs$g, na.rm = TRUE)) * 255
XA.pcs$b <- (XA.pcs$b - min(XA.pcs$b, na.rm = TRUE))/(max(XA.pcs$b, na.rm = TRUE) - min(XA.pcs$b, na.rm = TRUE)) * 255
XA.raster.r <- XA.raster.g <- XA.raster.b <- Env.stack.2[[1]]
values(XA.raster.r) <- XA.pcs$r
values(XA.raster.g) <- XA.pcs$g
values(XA.raster.b) <- XA.pcs$b
XA.rgbmap <- stack(XA.raster.r, XA.raster.g, XA.raster.b)

###Map spatial pattern of predicted genomic composition----
#Load geo coords
XA.coords <- read.csv("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/2_Chapter/2_2_Data/GeoSpatial/XA/XA_coords_Ch2.csv")

png("XA_Mapping_geographic_space_GF_BP_LF.png", width=800, height=1550, res=200)
plotRGB(XA.rgbmap)
points(XA.coords$lon, XA.coords$lat,pch = 21, lwd = 1.5, cex = 1, bg = "white")
dev.off()

XA.coords.shp=st_read("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/2_Chapter/2_2_Data/GeoSpatial/XA/XA_coords_Ch2.shp")

gnp <- st_read("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/1_Chapter/1_2_Data/GeoSpatial/Shapefiles/Grampians_W/Grampians_Wh_2.shp")

#hills <- raster("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/1_Chapter/1_2_Data/GeoSpatial/Climate_Raster/DEM_Grampians/hills.tif")

#dem <- raster("/Users/simon/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-DeakinUniversity/Thesis/3_Experiment/1_Chapter/1_2_Data/GeoSpatial/Climate_Raster/DEM_Grampians/GNP_dem.tif")

XA.coords.shp$Pop <- factor(
  XA.coords.shp$Pop,
  levels = unique(XA.coords.shp$Pop))

XA.rgbmap.spat <- rast(XA.rgbmap)

ggplot() +
  geom_spatraster_rgb(data = XA.rgbmap.spat) +
  geom_sf(data = gnp, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.5) +  
  geom_point(data = XA.coords.shp, aes(x = lon, y = lat, shape = Pop),
             size = 10, colour = "black", fill = "white",stroke = 1.5) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(25,
                                24,
                                22,
                                24,
                                24,
                                24,
                                21,
                                24,
                                24,
                                23,
                                22,
                                23,
                                22,
                                23,
                                23,
                                9,
                                9,
                                23,
                                21,
                                21,
                                21,
                                22,
                                25,
                                23,
                                24,
                                24,
                                25,
                                25,
                                22,
                                22)) +  
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) 

ggsave(file = "XA_GF_map.pdf", width = 12, height = 12, dpi = 300)

save(XA.rgbmap, file="XA.rgbmap.Rdata")
```

Procrustes residuals (Figure 4)
```{r Procrustes residuals}
#Load PCA objects for all species
load("BM.PCA.RData") #Banksia marginata
load("CR.PCA.RData") #Callitris rhomboidea
load("EI.PCA.RData") #Epacris impressa
load("EO.PCA.RData") #Eucalyptus obliqua
load("GA.PCA.RData") #Grevillea aquifolium
load("XA.PCA.RData") #Xanthorrhoea australis

#Convert raster stack into data frame
Env.stack.2.df <- na.omit(extract(Env.stack.2, 1:ncell(Env.stack.2), df = TRUE))

#Banksia marginata vs. Callitris rhomboidea----
BM.CR.diff.procrustes = procrustes(BM.PCA, CR.PCA, scale=TRUE, symmetrical=FALSE)
BM.CR.resids = residuals(BM.CR.diff.procrustes)

summary(BM.CR.resids)
#Min.   1st Qu.    Median      Mean   3rd Qu.      Max. 
#0.0001233 0.0036727 0.0048186 0.0051123 0.0063271 0.0249443 

#Specify raster properties based on an existing one
BM.CR.rastProc <- Env.stack.2$BIO05  

# Make all non-zero values in mask = 0.015
BM.CR.rastProc[BM.CR.rastProc != 0] <- 0.0249443  

#Assign residuals to raster cells
BM.CR.rastProc[Env.stack.2.df$ID] <- BM.CR.resids  

##Map the results----
pdf("BM.CR.ProcrustesMap.pdf")
plot(BM.CR.rastProc, col = sequential_hcl(23, "Lajolla"),
     main="BM vs. CR",
     font.main=4, font.lab=4, font.sub=4)
dev.off()

tmap_mode("plot")
BM.CR.rastProc.2 <-
  tm_shape(BM.CR.rastProc)+
  tm_raster(style = 'cont',
            n=6, 
            palette= "-RdGy",
            title="",
            legend.is.portrait = FALSE) +
  tm_shape(gnp) +  
  tm_borders("black", lwd = 1)  +
  tm_shape(BM.coords.shp) +
  tm_symbols(shape = "Pop",
             shapes = 22,
             border.col="black",
             col = "white",
             border.lwd=0.7,
             size = 0.5,
             legend.shape.show = F)+
  tm_shape(CR.coords.shp) +
  tm_symbols(shape = "Pop",
             shapes = 21,
             border.col="black",
             col = "white",
             border.lwd=0.7,
             size = 0.5,
             legend.shape.show = F)+
  tm_layout(legend.outside = TRUE,
            legend.stack = "horizontal",
            legend.outside.position = "bottom",
            legend.outside.size = 0.1, 
            legend.text.size = 1,
            legend.title.size=1.5,
            main.title = "BM vs. CR", title.size = 0.4, 
            title.position = c("right", "center"), 
            inner.margins = c(0.03, 0.05, 0.03, 0.05))

tmap_save(BM.CR.rastProc.2, filename = "BM.CR.ProcrustesMap_2.pdf")

#Banksia marginata vs. Epacris impressa----
BM.EI.diff.procrustes = procrustes(BM.PCA, EI.PCA, scale=TRUE, symmetrical=FALSE)
BM.EI.resids = residuals(BM.EI.diff.procrustes)

summary(BM.EI.resids)
#     Min.   1st Qu.    Median      Mean   3rd Qu.      Max. 
#0.0005361 0.0055550 0.0080495 0.0085714 0.0107536 0.0539762 

#Specify raster properties based on an existing one
BM.EI.rastProc <- Env.stack.2$BIO05  

# Make all non-zero values in mask = 0.015
BM.EI.rastProc[BM.EI.rastProc != 0] <- 0.0539762 

#Assign residuals to raster cells
BM.EI.rastProc[Env.stack.2.df$ID] <- BM.EI.resids  

##Map the results----
pdf("BM.EI.ProcrustesMap.pdf")
plot(BM.EI.rastProc, col = sequential_hcl(23, "Lajolla"),
     main="BM vs. EI",
     font.main=4, font.lab=4, font.sub=4)
dev.off()

tmap_mode("plot")
BM.EI.rastProc.2 <-
  tm_shape(BM.EI.rastProc)+
  tm_raster(style = 'cont',
            n=6, 
            palette= "-RdGy",
            title="",
            legend.is.portrait = FALSE) +
  tm_shape(gnp) +  
  tm_borders("black", lwd = 1)  +
  tm_shape(BM.coords.shp) +
  tm_symbols(shape = "Pop",
             shapes = 22,
             border.col="black",
             col = "white",
             border.lwd=0.7,
             size = 0.5,
             legend.shape.show = F)+
  tm_shape(EI.coords.shp) +
  tm_symbols(shape = "Pop",
             shapes = 24,
             border.col="black",
             col = "white",
             border.lwd=0.7,
             size = 0.5,
             legend.shape.show = F)+
  tm_layout(legend.outside = TRUE,
            legend.stack = "horizontal",
            legend.outside.position = "bottom",
            legend.outside.size = 0.1, 
            legend.text.size = 1,
            legend.title.size=1.5,
            main.title = "BM vs. EI", title.size = 0.4, 
            title.position = c("right", "center"), 
            inner.margins = c(0.03, 0.05, 0.03, 0.05))

tmap_save(BM.EI.rastProc.2, filename = "BM.EI.ProcrustesMap_2.pdf")

#Banksia marginata vs. Eucalyptus obliqua----
BM.EO.diff.procrustes = procrustes(BM.PCA, EO.PCA, scale=TRUE, symmetrical=FALSE)
BM.EO.resids = residuals(BM.EO.diff.procrustes)

summary(BM.EO.resids)
#     Min.   1st Qu.    Median      Mean   3rd Qu.      Max. 
#0.0002299 0.0058461 0.0077728 0.0079987 0.0096391 0.0438066 

#Specify raster properties based on an existing one
BM.EO.rastProc <- Env.stack.2$BIO05  

# Make all non-zero values in mask = 0.015
BM.EO.rastProc[BM.EO.rastProc != 0] <- 0.0438066

#Assign residuals to raster cells
BM.EO.rastProc[Env.stack.2.df$ID] <- BM.EO.resids  

##Map the results----
pdf("BM.EO.ProcrustesMap.pdf")
plot(BM.EO.rastProc, col = sequential_hcl(23, "Lajolla"),
     main="BM vs. EO",
     font.main=4, font.lab=4, font.sub=4)
dev.off()

tmap_mode("plot")
BM.EO.rastProc.2 <-
  tm_shape(BM.EO.rastProc)+
  tm_raster(style = 'cont',
            n=6, 
            palette= "-RdGy",
            title="",
            legend.is.portrait = FALSE) +
  tm_shape(gnp) +  
  tm_borders("black", lwd = 1)  +
  tm_shape(BM.coords.shp) +
  tm_symbols(shape = "Pop",
             shapes = 22,
             border.col="black",
             col = "white",
             border.lwd=0.7,
             size = 0.5,
             legend.shape.show = F)+
  tm_shape(EO.coords.shp) +
  tm_symbols(shape = "Pop",
             shapes = 23,
             border.col="black",
             col = "white",
             border.lwd=0.7,
             size = 0.5,
             legend.shape.show = F)+
  tm_layout(legend.outside = TRUE,
            legend.stack = "horizontal",
            legend.outside.position = "bottom",
            legend.outside.size = 0.1, 
            legend.text.size = 1,
            legend.title.size=1.5,
            main.title = "BM vs. EO", title.size = 0.4, 
            title.position = c("right", "center"), 
            inner.margins = c(0.03, 0.05, 0.03, 0.05))

tmap_save(BM.EO.rastProc.2, filename = "BM.EO.ProcrustesMap_2.pdf")


#Banksia marginata vs. Grevillea aquifolium----
BM.GA.diff.procrustes = procrustes(BM.PCA, GA.PCA, scale=TRUE, symmetrical=FALSE)
BM.GA.resids = residuals(BM.GA.diff.procrustes)

summary(BM.GA.resids)
#     Min.   1st Qu.    Median      Mean   3rd Qu.      Max. 
#0.0001207 0.0037061 0.0050771 0.0052679 0.0065288 0.0167860 

#Specify raster properties based on an existing one
BM.GA.rastProc <- Env.stack.2$BIO05  

# Make all non-zero values in mask = 0.015
BM.GA.rastProc[BM.GA.rastProc != 0] <- 0.0167860 

#Assign residuals to raster cells
BM.GA.rastProc[Env.stack.2.df$ID] <- BM.GA.resids  

##Map the results----
pdf("BM.GA.ProcrustesMap.pdf")
plot(BM.GA.rastProc, col = sequential_hcl(23, "Lajolla"),
     main="BM vs. GA",
     font.main=4, font.lab=4, font.sub=4)
dev.off()

tmap_mode("plot")
BM.GA.rastProc.2 <-
  tm_shape(BM.GA.rastProc)+
  tm_raster(style = 'cont',
            n=6, 
            palette= "-RdGy",
            title="",
            legend.is.portrait = FALSE) +
  tm_shape(gnp) +  
  tm_borders("black", lwd = 1)  +
  tm_shape(BM.coords.shp) +
  tm_symbols(shape = "Pop",
             shapes = 22,
             border.col="black",
             col = "white",
             border.lwd=0.7,
             size = 0.5,
             legend.shape.show = F)+
  tm_shape(GA.coords.shp) +
  tm_symbols(shape = "Pop",
             shapes = 21,
             border.col="white",
             col = "black",
             border.lwd=0.7,
             size = 0.5,
             legend.shape.show = F)+
  tm_layout(legend.outside = TRUE,
            legend.stack = "horizontal",
            legend.outside.position = "bottom",
            legend.outside.size = 0.1, 
            legend.text.size = 1,
            legend.title.size=1.5,
            main.title = "BM vs. GA", title.size = 0.4, 
            title.position = c("right", "center"), 
            inner.margins = c(0.03, 0.05, 0.03, 0.05))

tmap_save(BM.GA.rastProc.2, filename = "BM.GA.ProcrustesMap_2.pdf")

#Banksia marginata vs. Xanthorrhoea australis----
BM.XA.diff.procrustes = procrustes(BM.PCA, XA.PCA, scale=TRUE, symmetrical=FALSE)
BM.XA.resids = residuals(BM.XA.diff.procrustes)

summary(BM.XA.resids)
#     Min.   1st Qu.    Median      Mean   3rd Qu.      Max. 
#0.0001493 0.0036473 0.0050338 0.0052838 0.0065159 0.0314617 

#Specify raster properties based on an existing one
BM.XA.rastProc <- Env.stack.2$BIO05  

# Make all non-zero values in mask = 0.015
BM.XA.rastProc[BM.XA.rastProc != 0] <- 0.0314617

#Assign residuals to raster cells
BM.XA.rastProc[Env.stack.2.df$ID] <- BM.XA.resids  

##Map the results----
pdf("BM.XA.ProcrustesMap.pdf")
plot(BM.XA.rastProc, col = sequential_hcl(23, "Lajolla"),
     main="BM vs. XA",
     font.main=4, font.lab=4, font.sub=4)
dev.off()

tmap_mode("plot")
BM.XA.rastProc.2 <-
  tm_shape(BM.XA.rastProc)+
  tm_raster(style = 'cont',
            n=6, 
            palette= "-RdGy",
            title="",
            legend.is.portrait = FALSE) +
  tm_shape(gnp) +  
  tm_borders("black", lwd = 1)  +
  tm_shape(BM.coords.shp) +
  tm_symbols(shape = "Pop",
             shapes = 22,
             border.col="black",
             col = "white",
             border.lwd=0.7,
             size = 0.5,
             legend.shape.show = F)+
  tm_shape(XA.coords.shp) +
  tm_symbols(shape = "Pop",
             shapes = 24,
             border.col="white",
             col = "black",
             border.lwd=0.7,
             size = 0.5,
             legend.shape.show = F)+
  tm_layout(legend.outside = TRUE,
            legend.stack = "horizontal",
            legend.outside.position = "bottom",
            legend.outside.size = 0.1, 
            legend.text.size = 1,
            legend.title.size=1.5,
            main.title = "BM vs. XA", title.size = 0.4, 
            title.position = c("right", "center"), 
            inner.margins = c(0.03, 0.05, 0.03, 0.05))

tmap_save(BM.XA.rastProc.2, filename = "BM.XA.ProcrustesMap_2.pdf")

#Callitris rhomboidea vs. Epacris impressa----
CR.EI.diff.procrustes = procrustes(CR.PCA, EI.PCA, scale=TRUE, symmetrical=FALSE)
CR.EI.resids = residuals(CR.EI.diff.procrustes)

summary(CR.EI.resids)
#     Min.   1st Qu.    Median      Mean   3rd Qu.      Max. 
#0.0001146 0.0043914 0.0056430 0.0065614 0.0076427 0.0339226 

#Specify raster properties based on an existing one
CR.EI.rastProc <- Env.stack.2$BIO05  

# Make all non-zero values in mask = 0.015
CR.EI.rastProc[CR.EI.rastProc != 0] <- 0.0339226

#Assign residuals to raster cells
CR.EI.rastProc[Env.stack.2.df$ID] <- CR.EI.resids  

##Map the results----
pdf("CR.EI.ProcrustesMap.pdf")
plot(CR.EI.rastProc, col = sequential_hcl(23, "Lajolla"),
     main="CR vs. EI",
     font.main=4, font.lab=4, font.sub=4)
dev.off()

CR.EI.rastProc.2 <- tm_shape(CR.EI.rastProc)+
  tm_raster(style = 'cont',
            n=6, 
            palette= "-RdGy",
            title="",
            legend.is.portrait = FALSE) +
  tm_shape(gnp) +  
  tm_borders("black", lwd = 1)  +
  tm_shape(CR.coords.shp) +
  tm_symbols(shape = "Pop",
             shapes = 21,
             border.col="black",
             col = "white",
             border.lwd=0.7,
             size = 0.5,
             legend.shape.show = F)+
  tm_shape(EI.coords.shp) +
  tm_symbols(shape = "Pop",
             shapes = 24,
             border.col="black",
             col = "white",
             border.lwd=0.7,
             size = 0.5,
             legend.shape.show = F)+
  tm_layout(legend.outside = TRUE,
            legend.stack = "horizontal",
            legend.outside.position = "bottom",
            legend.outside.size = 0.1, 
            legend.text.size = 1,
            legend.title.size=1.5,
            main.title = "CR vs. EI", title.size = 0.4, 
            title.position = c("right", "center"), 
            inner.margins = c(0.03, 0.05, 0.03, 0.05))

tmap_save(CR.EI.rastProc.2, filename = "CR.EI.ProcrustesMap_2.pdf")

#Callitris rhomboidea vs. Eucalyptus obliqua----
```
